The work has involved taking apart the Baphuon monument's 300,000 sandstone blocks and piecing them back together. The 11th-Century three-tier tower is part of the Angkor complex which draws two million tourists a year.  ...    The BBC's Guy De Launey in Phnom Penh says the Baphuon was once among the finest of the great monuments of Angkor, but by the 1950s it was on the brink of collapse. A French-led team of archaeologists decided that the only way to save the temple was to take it apart, our correspondent says.
